items.verse: فَتَقَبَّلَهَا رَبُّهَا بِقَبُولٍ حَسَنٍۢ وَأَنۢبَتَهَا نَبَاتًا حَسَنًۭا وَكَفَّلَهَا زَكَرِيَّا ۖ كُلَّمَا دَخَلَ عَلَيْهَا زَكَرِيَّا ٱلْمِحْرَابَ وَجَدَ عِندَهَا رِزْقًۭا ۖ قَالَ يَـٰمَرْيَمُ أَنَّىٰ لَكِ هَـٰذَا ۖ قَالَتْ هُوَ مِنْ عِندِ ٱللَّهِ ۖ إِنَّ ٱللَّهَ يَرْزُقُ مَن يَشَآءُ بِغَيْرِ حِسَابٍ
items.verseEn: So her Lord accepted her with good acceptance and caused her to grow in a good manner and put her in the care of Zechariah. Every time Zechariah entered upon her in the prayer chamber, he found with her provision. He said, "O Mary, from where is this [coming] to you?" She said, "It is from Allāh. Indeed, Allāh provides for whom He wills without account."

items.verse: وَٱذْكُرْ عَبْدَنَآ أَيُّوبَ إِذْ نَادَىٰ رَبَّهُۥٓ أَنِّى مَسَّنِىَ ٱلشَّيْطَـٰنُ بِنُصْبٍۢ وَعَذَابٍ
items.verseEn: And remember Our servant Job, when he called to his Lord, "Indeed, Satan has touched me with hardship and torment."

items.verse: وَلَمَّا دَخَلُوا۟ مِنْ حَيْثُ أَمَرَهُمْ أَبُوهُم مَّا كَانَ يُغْنِى عَنْهُم مِّنَ ٱللَّهِ مِن شَىْءٍ إِلَّا حَاجَةًۭ فِى نَفْسِ يَعْقُوبَ قَضَىٰهَا ۚ وَإِنَّهُۥ لَذُو عِلْمٍۢ لِّمَا عَلَّمْنَـٰهُ وَلَـٰكِنَّ أَكْثَرَ ٱلنَّاسِ لَا يَعْلَمُونَ
items.verseEn: And when they entered from where their father had ordered them, it did not avail them against Allāh at all except [it was] a need [i.e., concern] within the soul of Jacob, which he satisfied. And indeed, he was a possessor of knowledge because of what We had taught him, but most of the people do not know.

items.verse: وَمَا مُحَمَّدٌ إِلَّا رَسُولٌۭ قَدْ خَلَتْ مِن قَبْلِهِ ٱلرُّسُلُ ۚ أَفَإِي۟ن مَّاتَ أَوْ قُتِلَ ٱنقَلَبْتُمْ عَلَىٰٓ أَعْقَـٰبِكُمْ ۚ وَمَن يَنقَلِبْ عَلَىٰ عَقِبَيْهِ فَلَن يَضُرَّ ٱللَّهَ شَيْـًۭٔا ۗ وَسَيَجْزِى ٱللَّهُ ٱلشَّـٰكِرِينَ
items.verseEn: Muḥammad is not but a messenger. [Other] messengers have passed on before him. So if he was to die or be killed, would you turn back on your heels [to unbelief]? And he who turns back on his heels will never harm Allāh at all; but Allāh will reward the grateful.

items.verse: وَلَقَدْ جَآءَتْ رُسُلُنَآ إِبْرَٰهِيمَ بِٱلْبُشْرَىٰ قَالُوا۟ سَلَـٰمًۭا ۖ قَالَ سَلَـٰمٌۭ ۖ فَمَا لَبِثَ أَن جَآءَ بِعِجْلٍ حَنِيذٍۢ
items.verseEn: And certainly did Our messengers [i.e., angels] come to Abraham with good tidings; they said, "Peace." He said, "Peace," and did not delay in bringing [them] a roasted calf.
Arise [to pray] the night, except for a little -

items.verse: يَـٰٓأَيُّهَا ٱلنَّبِىُّ قُل لِّأَزْوَٰجِكَ إِن كُنتُنَّ تُرِدْنَ ٱلْحَيَوٰةَ ٱلدُّنْيَا وَزِينَتَهَا فَتَعَالَيْنَ أُمَتِّعْكُنَّ وَأُسَرِّحْكُنَّ سَرَاحًۭا جَمِيلًۭا
items.verseEn: O Prophet, say to your wives, "If you should desire the worldly life and its adornment, then come, I will provide for you and give you a gracious release.

items.verse: أَءِذَا مِتْنَا وَكُنَّا تُرَابًۭا ۖ ذَٰلِكَ رَجْعٌۢ بَعِيدٌۭ
items.verseEn: When we have died and have become dust, [we will return to life]? That is a distant [i.e., unlikely] return."

categories.name: كَلِمَاتٌ عَلَى صِيغَةِ ٱلْمَبْنِيِّ لِلْمَجْهُولِ
categories.nameEn: Words in the passive form (verb or participle) describing a significant state or event

items.verse: وَقِيلَ يَـٰٓأَرْضُ ٱبْلَعِى مَآءَكِ وَيَـٰسَمَآءُ أَقْلِعِى وَغِيضَ ٱلْمَآءُ وَقُضِىَ ٱلْأَمْرُ وَٱسْتَوَتْ عَلَى ٱلْجُودِىِّ ۖ وَقِيلَ بُعْدًۭا لِّلْقَوْمِ ٱلظَّـٰلِمِينَ
items.verseEn: And it was said, "O earth, swallow your water, and O sky, withhold [your rain]." And the water subsided, and the matter was accomplished, and it [i.e., the ship] came to rest on the [mountain of] Jūdiyy. And it was said, "Away with the wrongdoing people."
And when the girl [who was] buried alive is asked
And at the earth - how it is spread out?
And when full-term she-camels are neglected

categories.name: كَلِمَاتٌ هِيَ الثَّانِيَةُ فِي آيَاتِهَا
categories.nameEn: Words that are the second word of their respective verse

items.verse: تَبَـٰرَكَ ٱلَّذِى بِيَدِهِ ٱلْمُلْكُ وَهُوَ عَلَىٰ كُلِّ شَىْءٍۢ قَدِيرٌ
items.verseEn: Blessed is He in whose hand is dominion, and He is over all things competent -
You are not, [O Muḥammad], by the favor of your Lord, a madman.
And [by] the day when it appears

harf.hint: A dwelling, an abode, a place of peace.
harf.arabicVerse: وَٱللَّهُ يَدْعُوٓا۟ إِلَىٰ دَارِ ٱلسَّلَـٰمِ وَيَهْدِى مَن يَشَآءُ إِلَىٰ صِرَٰطٍۢ مُّسْتَقِيمٍۢ
harf.verse: 10:25 — And Allāh invites to the Home of Peace [i.e., Paradise] and guides whom He wills to a straight path.

deduction.title: The Journey From the Depths
deduction.intro: A story of envy, patience, and divine planning unfolds around this notable figure. From the depths of despair to a position of great authority, this individual's life was a testament to trusting in Allah's wisdom, even when faced with betrayal from those closest. Can you identify this soul whose journey was guided by visions and vindicated by truth?
clues: I shared a vision I had with my father, which sparked jealousy among my siblings.
My own flesh and blood conspired against me, casting me away into darkness.
I was sold for a paltry price, becoming a servant in a foreign land.
I resisted a powerful temptation and chose prison over sin, declaring that my Lord is my protector.
My ability to interpret dreams, a gift from Allah, was the key to my freedom and brought me before the ruler of the land.
I devised a plan using a royal measuring cup to reunite with my youngest brother.

identity.label: Who Am I?
options: Prophet Yusuf
Prophet Musa
Dhul-Qarnayn
Prophet 'Isa
Prophet Dawud

trial.label: What Was My Trial?
options: Building a great ship to escape a flood
Being swallowed by a giant fish
Betrayal by siblings and false accusation
Confronting a king who claimed divinity
Leading an army against a giant's forces
trial.answer: Betrayal by siblings and false accusation

location.label: What Was a Key Element?
options: A staff that turned into a serpent
A coat of many colors
An ark of the covenant
A miraculous she-camel
A flock of birds that obeyed commands

outcome.label: What Was the Outcome?
options: The parting of the sea and destruction of an army
A city's destruction by a shower of stones
Being raised to the heavens alive
Forgiveness, family reunion, and leadership in Egypt
The construction of the Kaaba with a parent
outcome.answer: Forgiveness, family reunion, and leadership in Egypt

deduction.arabic: قَالَ ٱجْعَلْنِى عَلَىٰ خَزَآئِنِ ٱلْأَرْضِ ۖ إِنِّى حَفِيظٌ عَلِيمٌۭ
deduction.verse: [Joseph] said, "Appoint me over the storehouses of the land. Indeed, I will be a knowing guardian." (12:55)

translations: (to) his Lord Indeed, [I] has touched me
scramble.hint: This is a powerful supplication made by a Prophet known for his immense patience during a period of great trial.
scramble.arabic: ۞ وَأَيُّوبَ إِذْ نَادَىٰ رَبَّهُۥٓ أَنِّى مَسَّنِىَ ٱلضُّرُّ وَأَنتَ أَرْحَمُ ٱلرَّٰحِمِينَ
scramble.verseEn: And [mention] Job, when he called to his Lord, "Indeed, adversity has touched me, and You are the most merciful of the merciful."
